macro_segments.title: Introduction to Sukkah 25: The Mitzvah of Dwelling and the Framework of Exemptions

micro_segments.title: Overview of the daf and transition from construction to the mitzvah of sitting in the sukkah
Why the Mishnah begins with exemptions rather than the basic obligation
Enumeration of exemptions: shluchei mitzvah, cholim, eating and drinking temporarily
The foundational concept: basukot teshvu shivat yamim as living in the sukkah

macro_segments.title: The Principle of Osek B'Mitzvah: Source and Conceptual Debate

micro_segments.title: How do we know shluchei mitzvah are exempt? Source from Berakhot regarding Shema
The derech/discretionary activity interpretation: when you're not obligated to perform mitzvot
Two approaches to osek b'mitzvah patur min hamitzvah among the Rishonim

macro_segments.title: The Chatan Exemption: Mental Preoccupation and the Nature of Osek B'Mitzvah

micro_segments.title: Why is a chatan exempt from saying Shema? The apparent answer of distraction
Rashi's explanation: how preoccupation with the mitzvah comprises mental elevation
The distinction between virgin and widow: when preoccupation defines osek
Gemara's rejection of pure distraction theory; the avel counterexample
The distinction between tirda d'mitzvah and tirda d'reshut

macro_segments.title: Korban Pesach: A Second Source for Osek B'Mitzvah Patur Min Hamitzvah

micro_segments.title: The braita about those carrying Joseph's coffin and exemption from Pesach
Identification debates: Yosef's coffin carriers vs. Mishael and Eltzafan vs. anonymous burials
Why they became tamei on erev Pesach and couldn't purify in time

macro_segments.title: Korban Pesach: A Second Source for Osek B'Mitzvah Patur Min Hamitzvah (continued)

micro_segments.title: Why Korban Pesach case teaches osek b'mitzvah differently from Shema
Why both sources are necessary: Pesach for pre-encounter situations, Shema for in-the-moment conflicts

macro_segments.title: Application to Sukkah: Shluchei Mitzvah and the Compromise Model

micro_segments.title: How shluchei mitzvah exemption translates from Shema/Pesach to sukkah law
Tosafot's requirement that sitting/eating in sukkah must compromise the mitzvah
The alternative approach: osek is exempt even without compromise
Practical limits: when is something trivial enough that exemption doesn't apply

macro_segments.title: The Avel's Obligation in Sukkah: Tzaar and the Limits of Compassion

micro_segments.title: Rav Abba bar Zavda in the name of Rav: avel is exempt from tefillin based on glory
Why avel is obligated in sukkah despite apparent tzaar
The gemara's claim that avel brings tzaar upon himself and should settle his mind
Rashi's interpretation: mitzta'er is exempt only when sukkah is the source of tzaar
Alternative reading: gemara denies credence to avel's tzaar regardless of source
Ambiguity in the gemara regarding mitzta'er exemption and its scope
